title: Need ability to connect to two different backends simultaneously (as separate profiles)
author: ariccb-webvar
body:
I currently have a local hermes agent setup on my Mac Mini that is for my personal work, but I just deployed another hermes agent on a VPS that is connected to our company slack to give access for our company to work with the Hermes agent. 

I'd prefer to use the native mac desktop app for both - but it appears you can only choose to select one backend at a time, either local OR as a remote backend. 

It would be great to be able to use both and set them up so they are simply separate "Profiles" with different backends.

title: After installation, my fnm node config is overwrited.
author: john-data-chen
body:
I use fish shell and fnm to manage my node.js runtime, but Hermes Desktop installation will write a new node.js (22.x.x) over my fnm config in fish shell.

It is not hard to fix, I rollback fish config, then fnm runtime is back.
For web developers (JS ecosystem) like me, it is a big issue! Please fix it! And thank you for give us a such powerful app for Hermes Agent!

title: Silent infinite loop on fresh install ŌĆö missing lastConnectionID in preferences.json
author: Iamevanjordan
body:
## What I was doing
I finally got to the point of dispatching my first Hermes swarm(the latest update 0.15.1) to finish out some Temporal workflows and ended up on hermes-desktop (the native macOS app) after the web UI wasn't cutting it for my setup/workflow. After a fresh install I tried opening it up to connect to my VPS but every time I put in my credentials, then saved it to connect the app to my host, nothing appeared. No error, no spinner, no timeout message. Just sitting back at the main screen. 
## What I found out that happened
The app silently enters an infinite loop on launch when preferences.json is missing the lastConnectionID field. There's no UI feedback at all ŌĆö no error, no loading state, no indication anything is wrong. It just loops forever and you have no idea. This happens on a fresh install because preferences.json is created without lastConnectionID by default.
## Steps to reproduce 
1. Fresh install of hermes-desktop on macOS
2. Open the app ŌĆö it creates preferences.json if it doesn't exist
3. Add a connection via the UI (it gets saved to connections.json)
4. Attempt to connect to your host
5. App loops silently, no feedback, no timeout
## Expected behavior
Either auto-select the first/only connection, surface an error, or prompt the user to pick a connection. Anything but a silent loop.
## Actual behavior                                                                                                                                                                                
Silent infinite loop. Zero UI feedback.
## Root cause
preferences.json is missing the lastConnectionID key on a fresh install. The app appears to loop waiting for it without ever telling you that's the problem. preferences.json (broken state): json{"theme": "dark"} connections.json has your connection entry with an id field ŌĆö but preferences.json has no lastConnectionID pointing to it, so the app never knows which connection to use.
## Fix that worked
Manually inject lastConnectionID into preferences.json, matching the id of the entry in connections.json:json {"theme": "dark", "lastConnectionID": "your-connection-id-here"} Relaunch. App auto-connects immediately. Every subsequent launch works fine after that ŌĆö the field gets maintained once it's seeded.
## Why this matters
I'm not sure if I'm the first but new users on a fresh install may hit this wall every time. There's no error message, no log output visible in the UI, and no mention of this in the README. I spent longer than I'd like to admit ruling out VPS config, firewall rules, and SSH issues before tracing it to a missing JSON key lol.
---
Happy to help test a fix or take a crack at a PR if you can point me at the right place in the codebase. From what I've used it for so far I have been enjoying the overall app experience though!

title: Fish shell compatibility for CLI commands
author: aptonline
body:
The Hermes UI appears to execute generated CLI commands through the userŌĆÖs default shell. On systems using Fish (/usr/bin/fish), shell snippets generated with POSIX/Bash syntax fail.

Example output:

```
export HERMES_HOME="$HOME/.hermes";
export PATH="$HOME/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH";

if command -v hermes >/dev/null 2>&1;
then HERMES_BIN=$(command -v hermes);
else
  printf 'Hermes CLI not found.\n' >&2;
  exit 127;
fi;

COLUMNS=240 "$HERMES_BIN" skills list --enabled-only
```

Fish errors:

`fish: Missing end to balance this if statement`

Fish requires different syntax:

```
set -x HERMES_HOME "$HOME/.hermes"
set -x PATH "$HOME/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH"

if command -v hermes >/dev/null
    set HERMES_BIN (command -v hermes)
else
    printf 'Hermes CLI not found.\n' >&2
    exit 127
end

env COLUMNS=240 "$HERMES_BIN" skills list --enabled-only
```

Possible fixes:

* Detect shell ($SHELL)
* Generate shell-specific syntax
* Execute commands via sh -c instead of the userŌĆÖs login shell
* Provide Bash/Zsh/Fish adapters

Fish is increasingly common on macOS/Linux and this would improve compatibility.

title: Kanban board fails to load: no such column: session_id
author: philmcneal
body: FIXED In 0.9.1 THANK YOU VERY MUCH dodo-reach! ## Bug report The Kanban section in Hermes Desktop fails to load the remote Hermes Kanban board. From the UI, the error shown is: `Unable to load the remote Hermes Kanban board: no such column: session_id` ## Screenshot Attached screenshot shows the Kanban page failing to load. ## Environment - App: Hermes Desktop 0.9.0 - OS: macOS 15.7.4 (24G517) - Hermes profile: `default` - Hermes home: `hermes-home` - Section affected: Kanban - Version / commit: unknown ## Steps to reproduce 1. Open Hermes Desktop. 2. Select the `default` Hermes profile. 3. Go to the `Kanban` section from the left sidebar. 4. Wait for the Kanban board to load. ## Actual result The board does not load. The left panel shows: `Unable to load Kanban` With the detailed error: `Unable to load the remote Hermes Kanban board: no such column: session_id` The right panel still shows the empty task area and the `Create Kanban Task` button, but no board/tasks are loaded. ## Expected result The Kanban board should load normally, or Hermes Desktop should automatically migrate/update the Kanban database schema if the `session_id` column is now required. ## Notes This looks like a possible schema migration issue where the app is querying for a `session_id` column that does not exist in the current Kanban database/table. A clearer recovery path would also help, such as: - running the missing migration automatically, - showing a ŌĆ£repair/migrate Kanban databaseŌĆØ action, - or documenting the command needed to update the local/remote Kanban schema. FIXED In 0.9.1 THANK YOU VERY MUCH dodo-reach!

title: SwiftUI TextField does not accept Vietnamese IME input (accented characters)
author: quanglx2703
body:
## Description

Vietnamese input methods (e.g. Unikey, EVKey, macOS built-in Vietnamese Telex/VNI) cannot type accented characters into SwiftUI `TextField` components in Hermes Desktop. Characters with diacritics (├ó, ├¬, ├┤, ŲĪ, ─ā, Ų░, etc.) are either dropped, duplicated, or inserted incorrectly. The input method composition window may flicker or fail to commit characters.

This affects all text fields in the app: New Host form, session search, skill editor, and any other SwiftUI `TextField`.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Enable a Vietnamese input method (Unikey, EVKey, or macOS built-in Vietnamese Telex)
2. Open Hermes Desktop v0.9.0
3. Navigate to any text field ŌĆö try New Host dialog (Hermes ŌåÆ New Host)
4. Type Vietnamese text with accents ŌĆö e.g. "kß║┐t nß╗æi" or "quß║Żn l├Į"
5. Observe: accented characters do not appear correctly or are dropped

## Expected Behavior

Vietnamese Unicode characters with diacritics should be entered correctly, exactly as they work in native macOS apps (Notes, Safari, Terminal, Xcode) and AppKit-based text fields.

## Actual Behavior

- Accented characters are dropped or mangled
- The text field shows only the base character without the diacritic mark
- In some cases the composition window fails to commit the character
- Some fields swallow the IME input entirely

## Environment

- **App version:** 0.9.0 (also reproducible on earlier versions)
- **macOS version:** 15.7.5 (Sequoia)
- **Hardware:** Apple Silicon Mac
- **Input methods tested:**
  - Unikey (via EVKey engine)
  - macOS built-in Vietnamese Telex (C├Āi ─æß║Ęt ŌåÆ B├Ān ph├Łm ŌåÆ PhŲ░ŲĪng thß╗®c nhß║Łp liß╗ću ŌåÆ Tiß║┐ng Viß╗ćt)

## Root Cause (analysis)

SwiftUI's `TextField` on macOS has a known limitation with Input Method Editors (IME). Unlike AppKit's `NSTextField`/`NSTextView`, which properly implement the `NSTextInputClient` protocol, SwiftUI's text fields do not always handle the IME composition ŌåÆ commit lifecycle correctly for East Asian and Southeast Asian scripts (Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Vietnamese).

The terminal component (which uses SwiftTerm, based on AppKit's `NSTextView`) handles Vietnamese input correctly ŌĆö suggesting the issue is specific to SwiftUI's own `TextField` implementation.

## Workaround (temporary)

- Type Vietnamese text in another app (TextEdit, Notes) ŌåÆ copy ŌåÆ paste into Hermes Desktop
- Use the Terminal tab (SwiftTerm) ŌĆö IME works correctly there
- Add text via `hermes` CLI directly in Terminal

## Suggested Fix

1. Replace critical SwiftUI `TextField` components with `NSViewRepresentable` wrapping `NSTextField`/`NSTextView`
2. Or use a custom `NSViewRepresentable` that explicitly conforms to `NSTextInputClient`
3. Or consider using `.onReceive` with `NSControl.textDidChangeNotification` to properly handle IME commit events

title: [Show & Tell] hermes-memory-pgvector v0.3 ŌĆö Postgres + pgvector memory for hermes-agent backends
author: andreab67
body:
<!-- [Show & Tell] ŌĆö community announcement, not a bug report. Happy to convert to a Discussion if/when this repo enables them. -->

Hey ­¤æŗ ŌĆö sharing a memory-provider plugin that may be useful to folks running hermes-agent (and the desktop / workspace clients on top of it).

**`hermes-memory-pgvector` v0.3** ŌĆö drop-in Postgres + pgvector memory provider for hermes-agent. Stores hermes-agent's *built-in* `memory(action='add', ...)` writes into Postgres + pgvector with HNSW, and adds one `recall_memory(query, scope, target, limit)` tool for semantic search. No new ontology for the agent to learn, no LLM in the write path.

Key feature for multi-agent setups: **per-agent themes via `X-Hermes-Session-Key` header**, so your separate minions (marketing / trading / sre / etc.) don't pollute each other's recall by default ŌĆö and you can still `scope='all'` for cross-theme search.

Ships as a standalone plugin per hermes-agent's `CONTRIBUTING.md` (in-tree memory-provider directory is closed; new providers install into `~/.hermes/plugins/`).

- ­¤ō” PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/hermes-memory-pgvector/
- ­¤ÉÖ Repo: https://github.com/andreab67/hermes-memory-pgvector
- ­¤Æ¼ Full write-up + discussion: https://github.com/andreab67/hermes-memory-pgvector/discussions/1

Install:
```bash
pip install hermes-memory-pgvector
# then drop into ~/.hermes/plugins/pgvector/ and set memory.provider=pgvector
```

BSD-3-Clause. Tested live on a 7-minion ops stack. Feedback / issues welcome on the standalone repo.

ŌĆö Happy to delete or convert this Issue if it's not the right channel; I checked and Discussions are off here, otherwise I'd have posted there. ­¤ÖÅ

title: UX bugs: ANSI escape leak, touchpad scrolling, TUI lag, model label mismatch
author: ruangraung
body:
Hi. Tried Hermes Desktop today, love the vision! A native Swift macOS companion for Hermes Agent is exactly what I wanted. The UI feels right at home on Mac. Just wanna share a few rough edges from my session:

## Environment
- **macOS Tahoe 26.5:** MacBook Air M4 2025
- **Hermes Desktop:** latest release `v0.9.0` (downloaded May 21)
-  **Hermes Version:** `Hermes Agent v0.14.0 (2026.5.16)`
- **Connection:** SSH over Tailscale to remote VPS (Debian 13)

## Issues

### 1. ANSI escape codes leaking into chat UI
Random text like `55m;100m;20m...` appears in the message column during a chat session. These look like ANSI SGR sequence fragments where the `\e[` prefix was stripped but the number+m suffix remained visible. The text cannot be cleared or deleted, persists until app restart.

**Steps to reproduce:**
- Start a chat session via Hermes Desktop connected over SSH
- Send a few messages back and forth (especially ones with colored/formatted terminal output)
- Observe garbled text appearing in the chat area

### 2. Touchpad scrolling doesn't work
MacBook trackpad two-finger scroll is not supported in the chat view. The only way to scroll up through chat history is using the sidebar scrollbar, which exhibits erratic jumping/bouncing behavior, making it nearly impossible to read through past messages in a long session.

**Expected:** Standard macOS two-finger vertical scroll in the chat area.

### 3. Noticeable TUI rendering lag
When chatting through Hermes Desktop's embedded TUI, there's a perceptible delay compared to running Hermes TUI directly in Mac Built-in terminal or iTerm2 over SSH. The TUI output feels like it goes through an extra rendering/capture layer before appearing in the desktop UI.

**Likely cause:** The app captures/displays Hermes TUI output via SSH, and the ANSI-to-native-rendering pipeline introduces latency.

### 4. Model label doesn't update after live switch
When the model is changed mid-session (e.g., via `/model` or gateway config), the Hermes Desktop session UI and the Hermes Dashboard continue showing the previous model name. Only the live TUI correctly reflects the active model.

**Impact:** Session metadata is unreliable, the user and agent both see stale model info.

## Additional context

Connection is over Tailscale (direct peer-to-peer, confirmed via `tailscale status` as `active; direct`), so latency is not a network issue. The lag is reproducible and consistent across multiple chat turns.

## Suggestion

Consider whether the embedded TUI rendering can be streamlined, perhaps rendering the TUI output more directly instead of capturing/parsing ANSI-encoded output for native display. Also, a native chat input/display (instead of wrapping the TUI) would solve the scrolling and ANSI leak issues at the same time.

I know it's early days and these are polish issues, not fundamental ones. The concept is solid and I'm excited to see where this goes. The concept and native Swift approach is exactly right, just needs some UX polish to be a daily driver. Thanks for building this!  ­¤śŖ

title: macOS notifications for new messages and approval requests (inline approval UI exists but has no system notification)
author: cicae
body:
### Problem

Hermes Desktop currently provides **no macOS system notifications**, making it difficult to use when the app is in the background.

The approval UI **does exist** as an inline chat component (orange "Approval needed" box with Once/Session/Always/Deny options), but:
- No macOS notification banner is shown when an approval is requested
- No system sound plays
- No Dock icon bounce
- The app never called `UNUserNotificationCenter.requestAuthorization`

Similarly, when new assistant messages arrive, there is no notification ŌĆö the user must keep watching the chat window.

### Root cause of "never saw the approval dialog"

During testing, the first `rm` command triggered the approval inline dialog, but with no notification the user missed it. After the 60-second `approvals.timeout`, the agent auto-denied it. All subsequent similar commands were cached as "BLOCKED: User denied. Do NOT retry" and never reached the approval UI again. Only a **completely different command** (a later `rm` of temp files during unrelated auth setup) triggered a fresh approval dialog ŌĆö confirming the inline UI works, but the notification layer is missing.

### Expected behavior

1. **New message notifications**: When a new assistant message arrives while the app is in background, show a macOS notification banner with agent reply preview.
2. **Approval request notifications**: When a tool requires approval, fire a macOS notification AND call `NSApp.requestUserAttention(.criticalRequest)` for Dock bounce, so the user knows to return to the app.
3. **Configurable**: Allow users to toggle notification types in app preferences.

### Suggested implementation

- SwiftUI native: `UNUserNotificationCenter` for banners
- `NSApp.requestUserAttention(.informationalRequest)` for new messages
- `NSApp.requestUserAttention(.criticalRequest)` for approval requests
- `@AppStorage` settings to toggle each notification type

### Environment

- Hermes Desktop v0.8.1 (build 103)
- macOS 14+ (Apple Silicon)

title: Feature Request: Message arrival and approval request notifications
author: cicae
body:
### Problem

When using Hermes Desktop as a daily driver, there is currently no way to know that the agent has responded or that an approval is needed unless you are actively watching the chat window:

1. **No sound or banner when a new agent message arrives** ŌĆö The app is silent. If it's in the background or minimized, there's no way to know a response is ready.
2. **No notification when approval is requested** ŌĆö When Hermes Agent requires user approval for a command (e.g., ), the approval dialog/prompt appears silently. If you step away from the computer or have the app behind other windows, you will miss it entirely. The approval times out after 60s (configurable) and auto-denies.

### Current Workarounds

- Use the terminal CLI (which has  for audio feedback)
- Use Telegram (which has native push notifications)
- Constantly keep the app in the foreground

### Requested Features

#### 1. New message notification
When the agent finishes generating a response while the app is in the background or the user is on a different workspace, post a macOS notification center banner (and optionally play a sound) so the user knows to switch back.

#### 2. Approval request notification
When the Hermes Agent requests user approval for a command, the app should:
- Bounce the Dock icon ()
- Play a sound
- Optionally show a notification banner

This is especially important because approval requests block the agent from continuing ŌĆö without notification, the user may not know the agent is waiting.

### Implementation Notes (for developers)

This is a Swift/SwiftUI app, so the standard approach would be:

1. Request  authorization on first launch (or add a toggle in Settings)
2. On new assistant message arrival in the SSH session, post a  with  and 
3. On approval request, use  for dock bounce +  for audio feedback
4. Add a preferences toggle to let users configure: notification on message, notification on approval, sound on/off

### Environment

- App version: 0.8.1 (build 103)
- macOS: 26.5
- Hermes Agent: latest
- Connection: SSH (localhost)

---

*This is a feature request based on daily-driver usage. Happy to provide more details or test a preview build.*
